article_meta.title: Democracy Dies in DarknessVisual ForensicsThe ICE agent's cellphone video: Five key momentsNew footage sheds light on fa

reasoning: No attribution violations and some genuine author-prose bias flagged, but the model missed multiple clear biased phrases in the author’s own voice and had a couple of questionable labels. Output format also used markdown fences instead of a raw JSON array.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged text is from the op-ed author’s prose; no quoted-source language was mislabeled as author bias.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most items are defensible (travesty/headline, harder to make unhealthy choices, descent into sickness). The ‘Bias by Omission’ and ‘Flawed Logic’ items are more debatable but not egregiously wrong.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed numerous strong author-level biases: ‘bipartisan unifier?’; ‘finally’; ‘dead letter’; ‘No wonder...’; ‘justification enough’; ‘true win-win’; ‘good stewardship’; ‘duty to ensure’; ‘two crises’; praise lines including ‘make America healthy again’.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Several labels fit (Sensationalism, Unsubstantiated). ‘Opinion Presented as Fact’ for the headline is arguable but the article is explicitly labeled Opinion; ‘Bias by Omission’ and ‘Flawed Logic’ choices are questionable or could fit better under other types.
